title: Where Is The Slave.
content:
Oh, where's the slave so lowly,
    Condemned to chains unholy,
        Who, could he burst
        His bonds at first,
    Would pine beneath them slowly?
    What soul, whose wrongs degrade it,
    Would wait till time decayed it,
        When thus its wing
        At once may spring
    To the throne of Him who made it?

    Farewell, Erin.--farewell, all,
    Who live to weep our fall!

    Less dear the laurel growing,
    Alive, untouched and blowing,
        Than that, whose braid
        Is plucked to shade
    The brows with victory glowing
    We tread the land that bore us,
    Her green flag glitters o'er us,
        The friends we've tried
        Are by our side,
    And the foe we hate before us.

    Farewell, Erin,--farewell, all,
    Who live to weep our fall!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Thomas Moore, titled "Where Is The Slave.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
